patient.instructions: You are LiXX, a 62-year-old woman, anxious but speaking clearly. acceptable_variants.description: Selects a different guideline-consistent SGA (e.g., olanzapine with explicit metabolic-risk counseling, or quetiapine when clinically justified) while still completing contraindication and interaction checks prior to prescribing.
steps: Record diagnosis of early-onset schizophrenia.
Explain schizophrenia/psychosis and treatment with an antipsychotic.
Complete contraindication check and drug interaction check before prescribing; perform allergy check.
Prescribe chosen SGA with conservative dosing/titration and monitoring plan; document rationale for agent selection.
Establish follow-up and safety plan.

acceptable_variants.description: Initiates antipsychotic plus short-term management for severe agitation/insomnia (non-benzodiazepine preferred when possible) without deviating from required safety checks and without using additional WRITE tools beyond the two outcomes.
steps: Record diagnosis of early-onset schizophrenia.
Explain diagnosis and antipsychotic treatment.
Complete contraindication and interaction checks before prescribing; perform allergy check.
Prescribe SGA and provide adjunctive short-term symptomatic guidance (non-pharmacologic first; cautious pharmacologic options if needed) with clear warnings and follow-up.
Provide safety planning and close follow-up instructions.
